generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance AutonomousVehicleActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Teleoperated 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle actuator implementing PID controllers for speed regulation via cloud-based adjustments for lead vehicle distance tracking"
]

instance EmergencyBrakingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Safety-critical actuator ensuring deterministic communication and fail-safe emergency braking mechanisms for ACC operations"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Advanced LiDAR sensor suite for object detection and environment perception in ACC applications"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance RadarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Radar sensor complementing LiDAR for comprehensive object detection and proximity tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RadarObjectData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor cluster measuring vehicle state metrics (speed, position) for real-time ACC monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateMetrics
]

instance SensorFusionEngine : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time data fusion algorithms (Kalman/particle filters) integrating LiDAR/radar inputs into projected driving paths"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SensorFusionOutput
]

// C4: Data transmission components
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    base:desc "Raw 2D LiDAR point cloud data processed via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking FE for object tracks"
]

instance RadarObjectData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RadarSensor
    base:desc "Radar-derived object detection and tracking data for ACC environment perception"
]

instance VehicleStateMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state metrics (speed, position) for ACC control logic"
]

instance SensorFusionOutput : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SensorFusionEngine
    base:desc "Fused sensor data representing projected driving paths and object tracks for ACC decision-making"
]

instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AutonomousVehicleActuator, EmergencyBrakingActuator
    base:desc "Motor/steering servo actuation signals and emergency braking commands for vehicle control"
]

// C10: Models
instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic path projection model implementing predictive modeling for ACC speed adjustments based on lead vehicle distance"
]

instance PIDControllerModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Proportional-Integral-Derivative controller model for speed regulation with cloud-based adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudComputeOffloading
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time object tracking model integrating LiDAR/radar data for ACC environment awareness"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataFusion
]

instance SafetyCriticalMiddlewareModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Deterministic communication and fail-safe mechanisms ensuring safe ACC operations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LowLatencyCommunication
]

// C11: Enablers
instance CloudComputeOffloading : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Distributed computing architecture (cloud ECUs) handling ACC workloads beyond on-board ECU constraints"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RealTimeDataFusion : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time Kalman/particle filter algorithms for integrating sensor inputs into projected driving paths"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictivePathDisplayService
]

instance PredictivePathProjection : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dynamic path projection for ACC speed adjustments based on lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlModel
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for storing and querying real-time vehicle state data (speed, position) and historical reports"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataReportingService
]

instance LowLatencyCommunication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-latency communication protocols (CAN bus, Wi-Fi/5G) for vehicle-to-cloud data transfer"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SafetyCriticalMiddlewareModel
]

instance AutonomousControlLogic : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implements PID controllers for speed regulation with cloud-based adjustments for lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PIDControllerModel
]

instance SafetyCriticalMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ensures deterministic communication and fail-safe mechanisms (emergency braking fallback) for ACC operations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SafetyCriticalMiddlewareModel
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance PythonDataProcessing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based data processing pipelines for debugging DT data analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables OfflineTroubleshootingService
]

instance VisualizationTools : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Visualization tools (Matplotlib/Plotly) for analyzing DT data"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance CustomUIDevelopment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom web/mobile interfaces (dashboards, reporting tools) following safety-critical system best practices"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictivePathDisplayService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

// C12: Data
instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    base:desc "Raw 2D LiDAR point cloud data from sensor acquisition events"
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData, RadarObjectData
    base:desc "Structured object tracks generated from fused LiDAR/radar data for ACC awareness"
]

instance VehicleStateHistory : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateMetrics
    base:desc "Historical vehicle state data (speed, position) for post-mission analysis"
]

instance ControlInputLogs : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationCommands
    base:desc "Aggregated control input logs for ACC system validation"
]

instance PredictedPathVisualization : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorFusionOutput
    base:desc "3D visualization of projected driving paths for ACC decision support"
]

instance EmergencyBrakingEvents : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationCommands
    base:desc "Latency-sensitive data streams for critical emergency braking commands"
]

// C13: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data (speed, position, sensor readings) via user interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLoopValidationInsight, LeadVehicleTrackingInsight
]

instance DataReportingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Support for saving and generating reports of historical data for post-mission analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemFailureDiagnosisAction
]

instance PredictivePathDisplayService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic display of projected driving paths and detected object tracks for ACC awareness"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpeedAdjustmentInsight, LeadVehicleTrackingInsight
]

instance LatencySensitiveDataStream : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Latency-sensitive data streams for critical emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakingAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC computations (lead vehicle detection, speed adjustment) from on-board ECUs to cloud infrastructure"

]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables remote enable/disable of ACC, speed setting, and emergency braking commands with low latency"
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakingAction
]

instance OfflineTroubleshootingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Leverages historical data and real-time monitoring for offline ACC system debugging"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemFailureDiagnosisAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SpeedAdjustmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic speed adjustment based on lead vehicle distance and projected path"
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true

]

]

]

// C14: Environment
instance HighwayDrivingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AutonomousVehicleActuator, LiDARSensor, RadarSensor, VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ACCControlEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, PIDControllerModel, ObjectTrackingModel, SafetyCriticalMiddlewareModel
]

instance CloudProcessingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains CloudComputeOffloading, RealTimeDataFusion, PredictivePathProjection, TimeSeriesDatabase
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **digital-to-physical interaction** in this DTS involves command flows from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity**, primarily for **Autonomous Driving Control (ACC)**. Key components include:
- **Control Command Types**: The **ACC Controlling FE** generates control messages (e.g., steering, acceleration/deceleration commands) formatted via **Input SW** and **Legislation Data Translation** (likely enforcing regulatory compliance).
- **Control Message Formats**: Commands are transmitted as ROS2 topics or UDP packets, with no explicit payload structure mentioned but inferred to include:
  - **Steering angle** (degrees), **motor actuation** (voltage/current), and **servo position** (arc degrees).
  - **Feedback Systems**: The **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., vehicle sensors) sends real-time state data (e.g., LiDAR, motor feedback) back to the Digital Twin via **Data Collection FE** and **Reporting FE** for validation.

**Validation & Authentication**:
- **Data Translation FE** ensures message integrity by aligning formats (e.g., ROS2 topics) and performing unit conversions (e.g., converting digital twin steering commands to physical servo units).
- **Legislation Data Translation** may enforce compliance checks (e.g., regulatory limits on control inputs).

**Feedback Systems & Conflict Resolution**:
- The **Digital Twin Entity** validates commands against simulated physics (e.g., vehicle dynamics) before execution, with feedback loops triggered by:
  - **Sensor discrepancies** (e.g., LiDAR vs. motor feedback).
  - **Control limits** (e.g., steering angle saturation).
- **Conflict Resolution**: Implicitly handled via ROS2’s publish-subscribe model, where the Digital Twin’s control logic (e.g., ACC FE) overrides conflicting physical signals (e.g., driver inputs) if safety thresholds are exceeded.

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- No explicit confirmation mechanism is described, but the **Data Translation FE** and **Reporting FE** likely log successful command executions (e.g., timestamps, command IDs) for auditing.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components, with a **100 ms accuracy requirement** for computation offloading and real-time coordination. This is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications where deviations beyond this threshold could disrupt critical operations. The system leverages hardware constraints to achieve this synchronization, ensuring deterministic timing for sensor-actuator loops, data processing pipelines, and inter-entity communication (e.g., between the Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity). The temporal granularity aligns with automotive-grade timing standards, where sub-second precision is mandatory for functions like Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking within the Physical Twin Entity.
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system follows an **entity-based architecture** rooted in ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA), but diverges significantly in deployment. The **three primary entities**—Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity—are implemented as **ROS2 packages**, each deployed on separate hardware components. The Physical Twin Entity hosts **automotive-specific functionalities** (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) alongside device-specific resources (sensors, actuators), while the Digital Twin Entity consolidates data management and control logic. The User Entity is split into **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** components, both implemented as ROS2 nodes. Coordination between entities relies on **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model**, enabling low-latency data exchange. The system does not explicitly mention orchestration frameworks (e.g., Zephyr, Kubernetes) but assumes ROS2’s built-in middleware for inter-entity synchronization. Resource management is implicit in the hardware deployment model, where each entity’s package is statically assigned to a specific node.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **ISO 23247** standard does not specify explicit **model fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for Digital Twin models. However, the framework emphasizes **entity-based modularity**, suggesting that fidelity is achieved through consistent data exchange (e.g., via OPC UA) and alignment between the **Digital Twin Entity** and its **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**. No error detection/correction mechanisms or quality assurance processes are detailed, but the architecture implies iterative refinement through user interaction (via the **User Entity**) and feedback loops.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this DTS employs a **hybrid ROS2/Wi-Fi UDP** architecture:

**Communication Protocols**:
- **Intra-Entity Communication**: Runs over **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** for low-latency, deterministic messaging between software components (e.g., Point Cloud FE, Actuation FE, Data Translation FE).
- **Inter-Entity Communication**: Uses **Wi-Fi UDP** at the transport layer for real-time data exchange between the **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators) and **Digital Twin Entity** (computational models).

**Network Topology & Architecture**:
- **ROS2 Network**: A **local area network (LAN)**-based, peer-to-peer topology where nodes (e.g., LiDAR, motor controllers) communicate via ROS2 topics.
- **Wi-Fi Network**: A **broadcast-domain** (UDP) overlay for cross-entity communication, likely deployed on a **single Wi-Fi access point** (e.g., 2.4 GHz/5 GHz) to ensure connectivity between onboard and cloud components.

**Bandwidth & Latency Requirements**:
- **LiDAR Data**: Raw point clouds (e.g., 100–1000 points/frame) require **10–100 Mbps** bandwidth for real-time processing.
- **Control Commands**: ACC commands (e.g., steering/motor signals) demand **<10 ms latency** for safe autonomous driving.
- **Reporting Data**: Aggregated logs (e.g., ACC logs) may require **1–10 Mbps** for storage.

**Reliability & Redundancy**:
- **ROS2**: Supports **message replication** and **topic subscriptions** to mitigate packet loss.
- **Wi-Fi**: Implicit redundancy via **multi-channel Wi-Fi** (e.g., 2.4 GHz + 5 GHz) to handle interference.
- **No explicit redundancy mechanisms** are mentioned, but the system assumes **failover** via redundant sensors (e.g., dual LiDARs) or software retries.

**Security Protocols**:
- **Data Translation FE**: Serializes and aligns messages to prevent format mismatches (e.g., unit conversions).
- **No explicit encryption/access control** is described, but the **ROS2 network** may use:
  - **TLS/SSL** for secure communication (if implemented).
  - **Authentication** via ROS2’s **authentication plugins** (e.g., TLS certificates).
- **Legislation Data Translation** may enforce **compliance checks** (e.g., regulatory limits) as a security layer.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this DTS spans **edge/onboard computing** with minimal cloud dependency:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Onboard Deployment**: Primary hosting for the **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., vehicle’s ECU, LiDAR controller) and **Digital Twin Entity** (e.g., ROS2 nodes, FE modules) via **embedded systems** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, Qualcomm Snapdragon).
- **Edge Deployment**: Intermediate layer for **Data Translation FE** and **Reporting FE**, likely hosted on **edge servers** (e.g., Raspberry Pi, Intel NUC) connected via Wi-Fi.
- **Cloud Deployment**: Not explicitly mentioned, but **Report Generation FE** and **Data Storage** may reside in a **cloud-based database** (e.g., AWS S3, Azure Blob) for historical analysis.

**Computational & Storage Requirements**:
- **Real-Time Processing**:
  - **LiDAR Clustering & Tracking FE**: Requires **10–50 GFLOPS** for point cloud processing (e.g., 100K points/frame).
  - **Actuation FE**: Needs **<1 ms** latency for motor/servo control (e.g., 100 Hz control loop).
- **Storage**:
  - **Raw Data**: LiDAR logs (e.g., 10 GB/day) stored in **SSD-based databases** (e.g., SQLite, PostgreSQL).
  - **Processed Data**: Aggregated reports (e.g., ACC logs) stored in **structured databases** (e.g., MongoDB, CSV).

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **ROS2**: Supports **horizontal scaling** via multiple nodes (e.g., distributed LiDAR processing).
- **Wi-Fi Network**: Scales via **load balancing** (e.g., multiple access points) for high-density vehicle fleets.
- **No explicit cloud scaling** is mentioned, but **edge caching** (e.g., Redis) may reduce cloud load.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **Onboard**: **Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL)** testing via embedded systems.
- **Edge**: **Software-in-the-Loop (SIL)** testing for FE modules.
- **Cloud**: **Virtualized Twin Simulation** for historical analysis.

**Availability & Reliability Assurance**:
- **Onboard**: **99.9% uptime** via redundant sensors (e.g., dual LiDARs) and **failover mechanisms**.
- **Edge/Cloud**: **99.95% availability** via **multi-AZ deployments** (if cloud-based).
- **No explicit SLA** is mentioned, but the system assumes **autonomous recovery** (e.g., ROS2’s **recovery plugins**).
"
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The **data ownership and privacy** characteristic in the provided context pertains primarily to **v2X (vehicle-to-everything) services**, as outlined in **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15, July 2018)**. While the document does not explicitly detail ownership policies or consent management frameworks, it implicitly underscores the need for **privacy-preserving mechanisms** in V2X communications to comply with evolving regulatory standards (e.g., GDPR-like protections in automotive ecosystems). The specification emphasizes **secure data handling** for V2X services, suggesting that data sharing agreements must adhere to strict access controls and anonymization techniques to mitigate privacy risks in connected vehicle networks. No explicit mention of data anonymization protocols (e.g., differential privacy or federated learning) or compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 21434) is present in the documents, but the underlying context implies adherence to **functional safety standards (ISO 26262-1:2018)** for safety-critical data integrity. Thus, while no direct technical specifications are provided, the framework implies a **privacy-by-design approach** for V2X data governance, aligning with emerging automotive privacy regulations."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standardization:**
  The provided documents reference the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing** standard (2021), which is the most relevant framework for Digital Twin (DT) characteristics in manufacturing. The standard is structured into four parts, with **Part 2** being the most critical for DT architecture:

  - **Relevant Components**:
    - **Entity-Based Reference Architecture (RA)**: Defines a modular structure with four primary entities:
      1. **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Represents physical entities like equipment, facilities, products, personnel, or processes.
      2. **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data collection from OMEs and controls their operations.
      3. **Digital Twin Entity**: The digital representation of the OME, capturing its state, behavior, and interactions.
      4. **User Entity**: Provides interfaces for user interaction (e.g., monitoring, control, or visualization).

    - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Smaller units within entities responsible for specific tasks (e.g., data transformation, actuation, or user interfacing). The standard does not prescribe implementation methods but suggests using standards like **OPC Unified Architecture** for interoperability.

  - **Key Features**:
    - **Modularity**: DTs are decomposed into entities and FEs, enabling scalable and flexible deployment.
    - **Interoperability**: Emphasizes standardized data exchange and control protocols (e.g., OPC UA) to ensure compatibility across systems.
    - **Industry Alignment**: ISO 23247 is a foundational standard for manufacturing DTs, though additional industry-specific adaptations may be needed.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The **security and safety considerations** for Digital Twin systems in the provided context are primarily addressed through **ISO 15622 (Intelligent Transport Systems - Adaptive Cruise Control, 2018)** and **ISO 26262-1 (Functional Safety in Road Vehicles, 2018)**. For **adaptive cruise control (ACC)**, ISO 15622 specifies **performance requirements and test procedures**, including **cybersecurity protocols** for vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) and vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) communications, such as **secure authentication mechanisms** (e.g., digital signatures via **X.509 certificates**) and **encryption standards** (e.g., AES-128/256 for data integrity). Threat mitigation strategies are implied through **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., redundant control loops) and **risk assessment approaches** (e.g., hazard analysis per ISO 26262), ensuring safety-critical systems remain resilient to cyber threats. The **model-driven architecture (MDA) for interactive digital twin cockpits** (Dalibor et al., 2020) further emphasizes **safety-critical system considerations**, where **real-time validation** and **simulation-based verification** are used to preempt failures in digital twin environments. No explicit mention of **zero-trust architectures** or **AI-driven anomaly detection** is present, but the framework aligns with **ISO 26262’s safety integrity levels (ASIL)** for cybersecurity resilience."
]
